danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile creatures and obstacles in level environments
danger_signals.meaning: Areas containing threats that Bonk must avoid or defeat

danger_signals.visual_form: Boss character design and appearance as final level threat
danger_signals.meaning: Primary antagonist representing escalating danger and world threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Obstacles within prehistoric, dinosaur interior, Chinatown, and moon levels
danger_signals.meaning: Procedural dangers from level geography and themed hazards

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible items: candies, propeller seeds, flowers
safety_signals.meaning: Resources that enhance Bonk's abilities and survivability

safety_signals.visual_form: Hidden or accessible bonus stages throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones offering skill-based rewards and progression opportunities
Safe passage mechanisms between level areas

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Bonk character with distinctive head-based attack mechanic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Form-shifting through power-ups: Bonk Crab (shooting variant), Big Kronk (dinosaur form), size variants (tiny to huge)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence across multiple temporal/spatial settings: prehistoric levels, dinosaur interior, modern Chinatown, moon

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Arch nemesis character with distinct boss design
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Final confrontation point; represents unified threat across all world states

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ancient landscape design with primitive environmental assets
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Bonk engages with prehistoric-themed enemies and obstacles
Navigation within creature body; unique spatial and hazard logic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Contemporary urban architecture and cultural visual markers
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Platforming adapted to modern built environment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Low-gravity moon landscape with extraterrestrial visual design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Altered physics affecting movement and jumping mechanics

environmental_storytelling.example: Journey from prehistoric era → dinosaur interior → modern Chinatown → moon establishes escalating world-state change
Bonk's transformations (Crab, Big Kronk, size variants) communicate adaptive power progression and environmental mastery
King Drool's presence across all temporal settings as unifying threat creates coherent narrative arc
Flowers as collectibles suggest peace/growth; propeller seeds suggest mobility/freedom; candies suggest transformation/change

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Head-based attack mechanic; platforming through level hazards
Form transformation changes attack type (e.g., shooting as Bonk Crab) and movement capabilities
Tiny form enables access to restricted areas; huge form provides damage boost
Flight capability enables vertical exploration and hazard avoidance
Boss-level threat requiring accumulated power-ups and learned combat skills
Stationary or patrol patterns; defeatable through Bonk's head attack or power-up variants

notes: Super Bonk (Super Genjin/Super B.C. Kid) communicates world-state primarily through distinct visual level theming across four temporal/spatial environments. The game uses form-transformation as a core manifestation mechanic—power-ups visually distinguish Bonk's current capabilities and narrative progression. Danger is signaled through enemy presence and environmental obstacles specific to each world zone. Safety emerges from accessible power-ups and bonus level structures. The game employs no explicit faction color systems or political messaging; instead, it relies on protagonist-antagonist (Bonk vs. King Drool) polarity maintained across all environments. Environmental storytelling is achieved through thematic coherence within each level zone and the escalating journey structure. The head-based attack and flower-carrying mechanics introduce character-specific visual markers. Limited documentation of detailed visual communication systems in available source material.
